example: After a few drinks, he became maudlin and started crying about his childhood.
mnemonic: Magdalene(막달라 마리아)에서 유래 → 그림에서 항상 우는 모습으로 그려져서 감상적인

example: The court dismissed the lawsuit as frivolous and without merit.
mnemonic: '프리볼러스' → 'friv(가벼운)+olous' 가볍고 하찮은!

example: Her exemplary conduct earned her the Employee of the Year award.
mnemonic: '이그젬플러리' → 'example(예시)+ary' 본보기가 되는 모범적인!

example: The general's capitulation ended the war after months of fierce fighting.
mnemonic: capit(머리, 라틴어 caput) + ulation = 머리를 숙이는 것 → 항복. capit(머리): capital, captain, capitulate

example: The mournful dirge played at the funeral moved everyone to tears.
mnemonic: 라틴어 dirige(인도하소서, 장례 기도의 첫 단어). 장례에서 부르는 노래 → 장송곡

example: The dark clouds signaled the impending storm.
mnemonic: im(위에) + pend(매달리다, 라틴어 pendere) + ing = 위에 매달려 있는 → 임박한. pend(매달리다): pendant, suspend, depend

example: Could you elaborate on the details of your proposal?
mnemonic: e(밖으로) + labor(노동) + ate = 노동을 들여 만든 → 정교한. labor(노동): laboratory, collaborate

example: The sensuous texture of the silk fabric delighted her fingertips.
mnemonic: sensu(감각, 라틴어 sensus) + ous = 감각의 → 감각적인. sens(느끼다): sense, sensation, sensible

example: Her assiduous efforts in the lab eventually led to a breakthrough.
mnemonic: as(~에) + sid(앉다, 라틴어 sedere) + uous = 곁에 앉아 꾸준히 하는 → 근면한. sed/sid(앉다): sedentary, preside, reside

example: Her pallid complexion suggested she had been ill for some time.
mnemonic: pall(창백한, 라틴어 pallidus) + id = 창백한. pall(창백): pale, pallor, appall

example: The effulgent sunrise painted the sky in brilliant shades of gold.
mnemonic: ef(밖으로) + fulg(빛나다, 라틴어 fulgere) + ent = 밖으로 빛나는 → 찬란한. fulg(빛나다): refulgent

example: The new president received a strong mandate from the voters.
mnemonic: mand(명령하다, 라틴어 mandare) + ate = 명령하다. mand(명령): command, demand, mandatory

example: The professor expatiated on the causes of the French Revolution for two hours.
mnemonic: ex(밖으로) + pati(걷다, 라틴어 spatiari) + ate = 밖으로 걸어다니며 → 장황하게 논하다. spat(공간): space, spatial, expatriate

example: The human heart is analogous to a pump.
mnemonic: ana(비례하여) + log(이성, 말) + ous = 비례적으로 대응하는 → 유사한. log(이성): analogy, logic, dialogue

example: The pastor delivered a short homily on the importance of forgiveness.
mnemonic: 그리스어 homilia(대화, 설교), homilos(무리). 무리에게 하는 대화 → 설교

example: His solipsism prevented him from considering other people's perspectives.
mnemonic: sol(혼자, 라틴어 solus) + ips(자기, 라틴어 ipse) + ism = 자기만 존재한다는 주의 → 유아론

example: Winning the Nobel Prize represented the pinnacle of her scientific career.
mnemonic: pinn(뾰족한, 라틴어 pinna=깃) + acle = 뾰족한 꼭대기 → 정점. pin(뾰족한): pine(소나무=뾰족한 잎), pinnacle

example: The earthquake obliterated entire neighborhoods in seconds.
mnemonic: ob(완전히) + liter(글자, 라틴어 littera) + ate = 글자를 완전히 지우다 → 파괴하다. liter(글자): literature, literal, illiterate

ko: 형언할 수 없는, 말로 표현 못할
example: The beauty of the sunset over the ocean was ineffable.
mnemonic: '이네퍼블' → 'in(아닌)+eff(말하다)+able' 말할 수 없는!

example: The penurious landlord refused to fix the leaking roof.
mnemonic: penur(부족, 라틴어 penuria) + ious = 부족한 → 인색한, 가난한. penury(빈곤)

example: Freedom of speech is considered sacrosanct in democratic societies.
mnemonic: sacro(신성한, 라틴어 sacer) + sanct(신성한, 라틴어 sanctus) = 이중으로 신성한 → 신성불가침의

example: The family survived on an exiguous income that barely covered basic needs.
mnemonic: ex(밖으로) + ig(재다, 라틴어 agere) + uous = 재보면 거의 없는 → 빈약한

example: The scandal brought notoriety to the previously unknown politician.
mnemonic: not(알다, 라틴어 notus) + oriety = (나쁘게) 알려짐 → 악명. notorious(악명 높은)

example: The politician's mendacity was finally exposed by investigative reporters.
mnemonic: mendac(거짓, 라틴어 mendax) + ity = 거짓됨 → 거짓말. mendac(거짓): mendacious

example: He bore the contumely of his rivals with quiet dignity.
mnemonic: con(완전히) + tumel(부풀다, 라틴어 tumere) + y = 부풀어 오른 듯한 오만 → 모욕. tum(부풀다): tumescent, contumacious

example: Politicians who pander to extremists endanger democratic values.
mnemonic: 셰익스피어의 Pandarus(판다루스, 트로일러스와 크레시다의 중매쟁이)에서 유래 → 비위를 맞추다

example: The unscrupulous dealer sold fake antiques to unsuspecting tourists.
mnemonic: un(아닌) + scrupulous(양심적인) = 양심적이지 않은 → 비양심적인

example: Her mercurial temperament made it difficult to predict her reactions.
mnemonic: Mercury(수은, 수성) + ial = 수은처럼 빨리 변하는 → 변덕스러운

example: She found the prosaic details of office life incredibly boring.
mnemonic: pros(산문, 라틴어 prosa) + aic = 산문적인 → 평범한. 시(verse)의 반대로 산문은 평범하고 단조로움

example: Crepuscular animals are most active during dawn and dusk.
mnemonic: crepuscul(어스름, 라틴어 crepusculum) + ar = 어스름의 → 황혼의

example: The luminous stars lit up the clear desert sky.
mnemonic: lumin(빛, 라틴어 lumen) + ous = 빛이 있는 → 빛나는. lum/luc(빛): illuminate, lucid, translucent

example: The denigration of opponents has become a common tactic in political campaigns.
mnemonic: de(아래로) + nigr(검은, 라틴어 niger) + ation = 검게 칠하다 → 비방. nigr(검은): denigrate

example: The spy entered the country under the guise of a tourist.
mnemonic: 게르만어 wisa(방식, 모양). 겉으로 보이는 모습 → 외양, 변장. disguise(변장하다)와 같은 어근

example: He used circumlocution to avoid directly answering the question.
mnemonic: circum(둘레) + locut(말하다, 라틴어 loqui) + ion = 돌려 말하기 → 우회적 표현. loqu(말하다): eloquent, colloquial

example: A cabal of executives secretly plotted to oust the company's CEO.
mnemonic: 히브리어 qabbalah(카발라, 비밀 전승)에서 유래 → 비밀 결사, 음모 집단

example: His constant disparagement of her work eroded her confidence over time.
mnemonic: dis(부정) + par(동등한) + agement = 동등하지 않다고 함 → 폄하. disparage(폄하하다)의 명사형

example: The captious reviewer found fault with every minor detail.
mnemonic: capt(잡다, 라틴어 capere) + ious = 잡아내려는 → 트집 잡는. cap(잡다): capture, captivate, capable

example: The suspect was last seen driving a nondescript gray sedan.
mnemonic: non(아닌) + descript(묘사된, 라틴어 describere) = 묘사할 수 없는 → 특징 없는. scrib(쓰다): describe, script

example: The bumptious young executive annoyed his more experienced colleagues.
mnemonic: bump(부딪치다) + tious = 남에게 부딪치며 나서는 → 거만한, 건방진

example: The clear weather provided propitious conditions for the outdoor wedding.
mnemonic: pro(앞으로) + pit(구하다, 라틴어 petere) + ious = 앞으로 향하는 → 유리한

example: The intractable disease has resisted all known treatments.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + tract(끌다, 라틴어 trahere) + able = 끌려가지 않는 → 다루기 힘든. tract(끌다): tractable, attract, extract

example: These traditions are deeply entrenched in the local culture.
mnemonic: en(안에) + trench(참호) = 참호 안에 넣다 → 확고히 하다. trench(참호): trenchant(날카로운)

example: She had a visceral reaction to the gruesome scene in the movie.
mnemonic: viscer(내장, 라틴어 viscera) + al = 내장의 → 본능적인. 내장에서 느끼는 깊은 감정

example: The scholars engaged in a lengthy colloquy about the meaning of the ancient text.
mnemonic: col(함께) + loqu(말하다, 라틴어 loqui) + y = 함께 말하기 → 대담. loqu(말하다): eloquent, soliloquy, loquacious

example: The modern building looked incongruous next to the medieval cathedral.
mnemonic: '인콩그루어스' → 'in(아닌)+congruous(일치하는)' 일치하지 않는!

example: His jaundiced view of politics prevented him from appreciating any reform.
mnemonic: jaundice(황달, 프랑스어 jaunisse=노란). 황달로 세상이 노랗게 보이듯 → 편견을 가진

example: The landlord charged unconscionable rents to desperate tenants.
mnemonic: un(아닌) + conscion(양심) + able = 양심에 맞지 않는 → 비양심적인. sci(알다): conscience, conscious

example: The witness's incoherent testimony confused the jury.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + co(함께) + herent(붙은) = 함께 붙지 않은 → 일관성 없는. her(붙다): cohere, adhere, inherent

example: Despite all pleas, the obdurate judge refused to reduce the sentence.
mnemonic: ob(향해) + dur(단단한) + ate = 단단하게 굳은 → 완고한. dur(단단한): durable, endure

example: The tabloid published scurrilous allegations about the celebrity's private life.
mnemonic: scurr(어릿광대, 라틴어 scurra) + ilous = 어릿광대처럼 거친 → 상스러운. scurra(광대): 저속한 농담을 하는 사람
